tracks.title: Neon Hearts
tracks.loreNote: The 'safe' title track. Apex forced the band to include a callback lyric to their first hit to ensure brand consistency.

tracks.title: Hollow Sound
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's act of 'malicious compliance.' A 5:31 protest track explicitly calling out the studio's panic over the canceled 'Promise' single.

tracks.title: Side by Side
tracks.loreNote: The studio's furious overcorrection. An absurdly short, manic 1:54 'bubblegum' pop track demanded to fix the album's pacing.

tracks.title: If You Walked Away
tracks.loreNote: Marketed as a romance, this late-night radio ballad was actually Cassidy's genuine, terrifying plea to her brother not to abandon her.

tracks.title: Same Old Song
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's 'Trojan Horse.' A bright, optimistic keyboard hook masking a scathing critique of the studio's formulaic songwriting process.

tracks.title: Chain Reaction
tracks.loreNote: The start of the 'Side B Rebellion.' This pure rock track proved to the executives that the 'Engine' sound was what the audience actually wanted.

tracks.title: Not Your Doll
tracks.loreNote: Cassidy's arena rock anthem where she fiercely rejects the 'Stardust' persona Apex forced upon her.

tracks.title: Quiet on the Set
tracks.loreNote: The 'Punishment' begins. Apex forced Ryan to record this quiet, introspective ballad to kill the rock momentum built on Side B.

tracks.title: Shadow Dancing
tracks.loreNote: The studio strikes back. An atmospheric synth track where electric guitars were explicitly banned from the mix.

tracks.title: After the Show
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's final laugh. Forbidden from using 'pounding drums,' he recorded the rhythm by percussively slapping his acoustic guitar.

tracks.title: The Promise (Corporate Demo)
tracks.loreNote: The exact sound Apex wanted: safe, sterile, and ready for a televangelist broadcast. Scrapped after the 'Worship' video controversy.

tracks.title: The Promise (Sabotage Demo)
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's malicious compliance. He played the synth-gospel track but snuck a blistering, unholy rock solo into the bridge, terrifying the executives.
